17. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/NCCLUtils.cpp",
  "new_string": "// Wait for the communicator to be ready. This is a blocking function.\n// Arguments:\n//   longInterval: if true, wait with sleep of an interval; otherwise, wait\n//   with `sched_yield` which is faster (but acquires CPU more frequently).\nvoid NCCLComm::waitReady(bool longInterval) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (aborted_)\n    return;\n  // Inline polling with mutex already held.\n  // We already hold the mutex_ lock here, so the direct call to\n  // ncclCommGetAsyncError is safe.\n  ncclResult_t result = ncclInProgress;\n  auto startTimepoint = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();\n  auto timeout = nccl_nonblocking_timeout();\n  while (result == ncclInProgress) {\n    C10D_CHECK_TIMEOUT(startTimepoint, timeout);\n    if (longInterval) {\n      C10D_SCHED_SLEEP();\n    } else {\n      sched_yield();\n    }\n    ncclCommGetAsyncError(ncclComm_, &result);\n  }\n  if (result != ncclSuccess) {\n    std::string err = \"NCCL error in: \" + std::string(__FILE__) + \":\" +\n        std::to_string(__LINE__) + \", \" + ncclGetErrorWithVersion(result) +\n        \"\\n\" + getNcclErrorDetailStr(result, std::nullopt);\n    TORCH_CHECK_WITH(DistBackendError, false, err);\n  }\n}",
  "old_string": "// Wait for the communicator to be ready. This is a blocking function.\n// Arguments:\n//   longInterval: if true, wait with sleep of an interval; otherwise, wait\n//   with `sched_yield` which is faster (but acquires CPU more frequently).\nvoid NCCLComm::waitReady(bool longInterval) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (aborted_)\n    return;\n  // If timeout is reached, throw an exception.\n  if (longInterval) {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK_TIMEOUT_SLEEP(ncclInProgress, ncclComm_, std::nullopt);\n  } else {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK_TIMEOUT(ncclInProgress, ncclComm_, std::nullopt);\n  }\n}",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/NCCLUtils.cpp",
  "newString": "// Wait for the communicator to be ready. This is a blocking function.\n// Arguments:\n//   longInterval: if true, wait with sleep of an interval; otherwise, wait\n//   with `sched_yield` which is faster (but acquires CPU more frequently).\nvoid NCCLComm::waitReady(bool longInterval) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (aborted_)\n    return;\n  // Inline polling with mutex already held.\n  // We already hold the mutex_ lock here, so the direct call to\n  // ncclCommGetAsyncError is safe.\n  ncclResult_t result = ncclInProgress;\n  auto startTimepoint = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();\n  auto timeout = nccl_nonblocking_timeout();\n  while (result == ncclInProgress) {\n    C10D_CHECK_TIMEOUT(startTimepoint, timeout);\n    if (longInterval) {\n      C10D_SCHED_SLEEP();\n    } else {\n      sched_yield();\n    }\n    ncclCommGetAsyncError(ncclComm_, &result);\n  }\n  if (result != ncclSuccess) {\n    std::string err = \"NCCL error in: \" + std::string(__FILE__) + \":\" +\n        std::to_string(__LINE__) + \", \" + ncclGetErrorWithVersion(result) +\n        \"\\n\" + getNcclErrorDetailStr(result, std::nullopt);\n    TORCH_CHECK_WITH(DistBackendError, false, err);\n  }\n}",
  "oldString": "// Wait for the communicator to be ready. This is a blocking function.\n// Arguments:\n//   longInterval: if true, wait with sleep of an interval; otherwise, wait\n//   with `sched_yield` which is faster (but acquires CPU more frequently).\nvoid NCCLComm::waitReady(bool longInterval) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (aborted_)\n    return;\n  // If timeout is reached, throw an exception.\n  if (longInterval) {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK_TIMEOUT_SLEEP(ncclInProgress, ncclComm_, std::nullopt);\n  } else {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK_TIMEOUT(ncclInProgress, ncclComm_, std::nullopt);\n  }\n}",
  "originalFile": "#include <torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/NCCLUtils.hpp>\n\n#ifdef USE_C10D_NCCL\n#include <fmt/format.h>\n#include <mutex>\n#include <thread>\n#include <vector>\n\nnamespace c10d {\n\nNCCLComm::NCCLComm(ncclComm_t ncclComm) : ncclComm_(ncclComm) {}\n\nNCCLComm::~NCCLComm() noexcept {\n  // (kwen2501) Making CUDA/NCCL calls in this destructor can hit CUDA driver\n  // shutdown error if CUDA context has exited first. Thus, we are not\n  // destroying or aborting NCCL communicators here. We just detect and warn\n  // about the risk of memory leak. Normally, a user would have called\n  // `destroy_process_group` or `abort_process_group`, and such risk would be\n  // avoided.\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (ncclComm_ && initialized_ && !aborted_) {\n    TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\n        \"WARNING: NCCL communicator hasn't been destroyed. This may cause \"\n        \"memory leaks. To avoid the risk, you can call `destroy_process_group` \"\n        \"during normal exit or `_abort_process_group` when handling failures.\")\n  }\n}\n\n// NOLINTNEXTLINE(*-noexcept-move-*)\nNCCLComm::NCCLComm(NCCLComm&& other) {\n  // Using other's lock, as it reads other's states\n  // Can not use this.mutex_, as this object is being constructed.\n  LockType lock(other.mutex_);\n  std::swap(ncclComm_, other.ncclComm_);\n  std::swap(aborted_, other.aborted_);\n  std::swap(ncclAsyncErr_, other.ncclAsyncErr_);\n  std::swap(initialized_, other.initialized_);\n  std::swap(nonBlocking_, other.nonBlocking_);\n  std::swap(deviceIndex_, other.deviceIndex_);\n}\n\nvoid NCCLComm::setUniqueHash(ncclUniqueId ncclId) {\n  const uint8_t* bytes = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(&ncclId);\n\n  fmt::memory_buffer buf;\n  buf.reserve(NCCL_UNIQUE_ID_BYTES * 2); // 2 hex chars per byte\n  for (int i = 0; i < NCCL_UNIQUE_ID_BYTES; ++i) {\n    fmt::format_to(\n        std::back_inserter(buf), \"{:02x}\", static_cast<int>(bytes[i]));\n  }\n  this->uniqueHash_ = fmt::to_string(buf);\n}\n\nvoid NCCLComm::setUniqueHash(std::string hash) {\n  this->uniqueHash_ = std::move(hash);\n}\n\nstd::string NCCLComm::getUniqueHash() {\n  return uniqueHash_;\n}\n\nstd::shared_ptr<NCCLComm> NCCLComm::create(\n    int numRanks,\n    int rank,\n    ncclUniqueId commId,\n    at::DeviceIndex deviceIndex) {\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(deviceIndex);\n  auto comm = std::make_shared<NCCLComm>();\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n      ncclCommInitRank(&(comm->ncclComm_), numRanks, commId, rank),\n      std::nullopt);\n  comm->setUniqueHash(commId);\n  comm->rank_ = rank;\n  comm->deviceIndex_ = deviceIndex;\n  comm->initialized_ = true;\n  // Old style comm is always blocking.\n  comm->nonBlocking_ = false;\n  return comm;\n}\n\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_CONFIG\nstd::shared_ptr<NCCLComm> NCCLComm::create(\n    int numRanks,\n    int rank,\n    ncclUniqueId commId,\n    at::DeviceIndex deviceIndex,\n    ncclConfig_t& config) {\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(deviceIndex);\n  auto comm = std::make_shared<NCCLComm>();\n  comm->nonBlocking_ = config.blocking == 0;\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << rank << \": creating NCCL communicator with mode: \"\n            << (comm->nonBlocking_ ? \"nonblocking\" : \"blocking\");\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_NONBLOCKING(\n      ncclCommInitRankConfig(\n          &(comm->ncclComm_), numRanks, commId, rank, &config),\n      std::nullopt);\n  comm->setUniqueHash(commId);\n  comm->rank_ = rank;\n  comm->deviceIndex_ = deviceIndex;\n  // Under blocking mode, comm is initialized immediately after NCCL init\n  // returns; Under nonblocking mode, we check whether comm is initialized the\n  // *next* time ncclComm_ is accessed.\n  comm->initialized_ = !comm->nonBlocking_;\n  return comm;\n}\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_INIT_RANK_SCALABLE\nstd::shared_ptr<NCCLComm> NCCLComm::create_scalable(\n    int numRanks,\n    int rank,\n    std::vector<ncclUniqueId>& commIds,\n    at::DeviceIndex deviceIndex,\n    ncclConfig_t& config) {\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(deviceIndex);\n  auto comm = std::make_shared<NCCLComm>();\n  comm->nonBlocking_ = config.blocking == 0;\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << rank << \": creating NCCL communicator with mode: \"\n            << (comm->nonBlocking_ ? \"nonblocking\" : \"blocking\")\n            << \" with scalable init.\";\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_NONBLOCKING(\n      ncclCommInitRankScalable(\n          &(comm->ncclComm_),\n          numRanks,\n          rank,\n          commIds.size(),\n          commIds.data(),\n          &config),\n      std::nullopt);\n  // Only the first ncclUniqueId will be used to create the\n  // communicator hash id, which is used to identify the communicator\n  // in the log file and in the replay tool.\n  comm->setUniqueHash(commIds[0]);\n  comm->rank_ = rank;\n  comm->deviceIndex_ = deviceIndex;\n  comm->initialized_ = !comm->nonBlocking_;\n  return comm;\n}\n#endif // NCCL_HAS_INIT_RANK_SCALABLE\n#endif // NCCL_HAS_CONFIG\n\nncclComm_t NCCLComm::getNcclComm() {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (aborted_) {\n    auto commFailureMsg = commFailureReason_ != std::nullopt\n        ? c10::str(\" Original reason for failure was: \", *commFailureReason_)\n        : \"\";\n    TORCH_CHECK_WITH(\n        DistBackendError,\n        false,\n        c10::str(\n            \"NCCL communicator was aborted on rank \",\n            rank_,\n            \". \",\n            commFailureMsg));\n  }\n  // In non-blocking mode, ensure comm is ready.\n  if (nonBlocking_) {\n    // Wait with long interval if communicator is being initialized.\n    bool longInterval = !initialized_;\n    waitReady(longInterval);\n    // ncclComm_ should be initialized by now\n  }\n  if (!initialized_) {\n    // TODO: see if we can consolidate other `initialized_` flipping here.\n    // Maintaining it elsewhere is some work.\n    initialized_ = true;\n    LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << rank_ << \": NCCL communicator \" << repr()\n              << \" is initialized.\";\n  }\n  return ncclComm_;\n}\n\nat::DeviceIndex NCCLComm::getDeviceIndex() {\n  return deviceIndex_;\n}\n\n// Wait for the communicator to be ready. This is a blocking function.\n// Arguments:\n//   longInterval: if true, wait with sleep of an interval; otherwise, wait\n//   with `sched_yield` which is faster (but acquires CPU more frequently).\nvoid NCCLComm::waitReady(bool longInterval) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (aborted_)\n    return;\n  // If timeout is reached, throw an exception.\n  if (longInterval) {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK_TIMEOUT_SLEEP(ncclInProgress, ncclComm_, std::nullopt);\n  } else {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK_TIMEOUT(ncclInProgress, ncclComm_, std::nullopt);\n  }\n}\n\nstd::optional<std::string> NCCLComm::getNcclCommFailureReason() const {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  return commFailureReason_;\n}\n\n#if defined(NCCL_HAS_COMM_SPLIT)\nstd::shared_ptr<NCCLComm> NCCLComm::split(\n    NCCLComm* source,\n    int color_id,\n    int rank,\n    ncclConfig_t& config) {\n  TORCH_CHECK(\n      color_id >= NCCL_SPLIT_NOCOLOR,\n      \"Color must be a non-negative value or NCCL_SPLIT_NOCOLOR (-1)\"\n      \", but got \",\n      color_id);\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << source->rank_ << \": split from parent comm \"\n            << source->repr() << \" with color_id \" << color_id << \" and rank \"\n            << rank;\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(source->deviceIndex_);\n  auto comm = std::make_shared<NCCLComm>();\n  // This call will block until the source communicator is initialized\n  auto sourceComm = source->getNcclComm();\n#ifndef NCCL_HAS_COMM_NONBLOCKING\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n      ncclCommSplit(sourceComm, color_id, rank, &(comm->ncclComm_), &config),\n      std::nullopt);\n#else\n  // After calling ncclCommSplit in non-blocking mode, we should wait for the\n  // source communicator to be out of ncclInProgress state.\n  // Reason 1:\n  //   it's unsafe to call new operations on the parent comm while it's in\n  //   ncclInProgress state.\n  // Reason 2:\n  //   as of NCCL 2.23, the ptr value of child comm will not be filled until the\n  //   state of parent comm is ncclSuccess. This may change in the future. See:\n  //   https://github.com/NVIDIA/nccl/issues/1472\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_TIMEOUT_SLEEP(\n      ncclCommSplit(sourceComm, color_id, rank, &(comm->ncclComm_), &config),\n      sourceComm, // wait on parent comm\n      std::nullopt);\n  if (color_id >= 0) {\n    // Waiting for parent comm above still does not seem to guarantee the child\n    // comm ptr is valid. Therefore we add a manual wait here for safety.\n    // TODO: remove this wait after NCCL fix the semantics.\n    auto startTime = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();\n    auto timeout = nccl_nonblocking_timeout();\n    while (!comm->ncclComm_) {\n      C10D_CHECK_TIMEOUT(startTime, timeout);\n      C10D_SCHED_SLEEP();\n    }\n  }\n  // comm->ncclComm_ should have valid ptr by now, but not necessarily\n  // initialized. Rely on getNcclComm() to wait for its initialization.\n#endif\n  ++source->ncclCommSplitCounter_;\n  comm->rank_ = rank;\n  // Child comm should be on the same device as parent comm\n  comm->deviceIndex_ = source->deviceIndex_;\n  comm->nonBlocking_ = config.blocking == 0;\n  comm->setUniqueHash(\n      source->getUniqueHash() + \":\" +\n      std::to_string(source->ncclCommSplitCounter_));\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << source->rank_ << \": created child comm \"\n            << comm->repr() << \" with color_id \" << color_id;\n  return comm;\n}\n#endif\n\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_COMM_SHRINK\nstd::shared_ptr<NCCLComm> NCCLComm::shrink(\n    NCCLComm* source,\n    std::vector<int>& ranks_to_exclude,\n    ncclConfig_t* config,\n    int shrinkFlags) {\n  // Preconditions are validated in ProcessGroupNCCL::shrink\n\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << source->rank_ << \": shrinking comm \" << source->repr()\n            << \" excluding \" << ranks_to_exclude.size() << \" ranks\";\n\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(source->deviceIndex_);\n  auto comm = std::make_shared<NCCLComm>();\n\n  // This call will block until the source communicator is initialized\n  auto sourceComm = source->getNcclComm();\n\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_NONBLOCKING(\n      ncclCommShrink(\n          sourceComm,\n          ranks_to_exclude.data(),\n          ranks_to_exclude.size(),\n          reinterpret_cast<ncclComm_t*>(&(comm->ncclComm_)),\n          config,\n          shrinkFlags),\n      source->getNcclCommFailureReason());\n\n  // Wait for the child communicator to be ready\n  source->waitReady(true);\n  comm->initialized_ = true;\n\n  // NCCL automatically assigns rank during shrink - query it efficiently\n  int assigned_rank;\n  try {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n        ncclCommUserRank(comm->ncclComm_, &assigned_rank), std::nullopt);\n    comm->rank_ = assigned_rank;\n  } catch (const std::exception& e) {\n    // Fallback: if ncclCommUserRank fails, we can't determine the rank\n    LOG(ERROR) << \"Failed to query NCCL-assigned rank: \" << e.what();\n    throw;\n  }\n\n  // Child comm should be on the same device as parent comm\n  comm->deviceIndex_ = source->deviceIndex_;\n  if (config != nullptr) {\n    comm->nonBlocking_ = config->blocking == 0;\n  } else {\n    // Inherit parent behavior if no config provided\n    comm->nonBlocking_ = source->nonBlocking_;\n  }\n\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << source->rank_ << \": created shrunken comm \"\n            << comm->repr() << \" with NCCL-assigned rank \" << assigned_rank;\n\n  return comm;\n}\n#endif\n\nvoid NCCLComm::finalize() {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (aborted_) {\n    LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << rank_\n              << \": NCCL communicator already Invalidated. Skip finalize.\";\n    return;\n  }\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(deviceIndex_);\n  auto comm = getNcclComm();\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_NONBLOCKING(ncclCommFinalize(comm), std::nullopt);\n}\n\nvoid NCCLComm::destroy() {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (aborted_) {\n    LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << rank_\n              << \": NCCL communicator already Invalidated. Skip destroy.\";\n    return;\n  }\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(deviceIndex_);\n  auto comm = getNcclComm();\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(ncclCommDestroy(comm), std::nullopt);\n  // Poison future getNcclComm\n  aborted_ = true;\n}\n\nvoid NCCLComm::abort(std::optional<std::string> commFailureReason) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(deviceIndex_);\n#ifdef ENABLE_NCCL_ERROR_CHECKING\n  if (aborted_ && !initialized_) {\n    // Should not abort twice.\n    return;\n  }\n\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_COMM_REGISTER\n  // Deregister all registered segments before aborting.\n  for (auto& it : registeredSegmentHandles_) {\n    void* handle = it.second;\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n        ::ncclCommDeregister(ncclComm_, handle),\n        c10::str(\n            \"Failed to deregister segment handle \",\n            handle,\n            \" on ncclComm_ \",\n            ncclComm_));\n  }\n  registeredSegmentHandles_.clear();\n#endif\n\n  // Set true failure reason if provided by ProcessGroupNCCL (e.g. work\n  // timeout)\n  commFailureReason_ = commFailureReason;\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Aborting ncclComm_ \" << ncclComm_ << \" with reason: \"\n            << (commFailureReason ? *commFailureReason\n                                  : \"No abort reason provided.\");\n#ifndef NCCL_HAS_COMM_NONBLOCKING\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(::ncclCommAbort(ncclComm_), commFailureReason_);\n#else\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_TIMEOUT(\n      ::ncclCommAbort(ncclComm_), ncclComm_, commFailureReason_);\n#endif\n  aborted_ = true;\n  ncclComm_ = nullptr;\n\n  // Set an appropriate error so that we avoid using the communicator.\n  if (ncclAsyncErr_ == ncclSuccess) {\n    ncclAsyncErr_ = ncclSystemError;\n  }\n#else\n  // This is a NOOP, if error checks are disabled.\n  return;\n#endif\n}\n\nbool NCCLComm::isInitialized() const {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  return initialized_;\n}\n\nbool NCCLComm::isAborted() const {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  return aborted_;\n}\n\nuint64_t NCCLComm::getCommSplitCounter() const {\n  return ncclCommSplitCounter_;\n}\n\nncclResult_t NCCLComm::checkForNcclError() {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n#ifdef ENABLE_NCCL_ERROR_CHECKING\n  if (ncclAsyncErr_ != ncclSuccess) {\n    return ncclAsyncErr_;\n  }\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n      ncclCommGetAsyncError(ncclComm_, &ncclAsyncErr_), commFailureReason_);\n  return ncclAsyncErr_;\n#else\n  // Always return success, if error checks are disabled.\n  return ncclSuccess;\n#endif\n}\n\nncclResult_t NCCLComm::getAsyncError(ncclResult_t* asyncError) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  return ncclCommGetAsyncError(ncclComm_, asyncError);\n}\n\nncclResult_t NCCLComm::registerSegment(\n    void* ptr,\n    size_t size,\n    bool errorOnRereg, /*=true*/\n    bool window /*=false*/) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_COMM_REGISTER\n  // We register only segments from cache allocator\n  // which are guaranteed to be with disjoint addr ranges. Thus, a ptr always\n  // maps to a unique handle and should not be registered before the current\n  // ptr is deregistered and freed.\n  if (registeredSegmentHandles_.count(ptr) > 0) {\n    TORCH_CHECK(\n        !errorOnRereg,\n        \"Segment with ptr \",\n        ptr,\n        \" has already been registered on ncclComm_ \",\n        ncclComm_);\n    // Skip below\n    return ncclSuccess;\n  }\n\n  void* handle = nullptr;\n  // Use getNcclComm to make sure comm is ready before calling nccl APIs\n  auto comm = getNcclComm();\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_COMM_WINDOW_REGISTER\n  if (window) {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n        ncclCommWindowRegister(\n            comm, ptr, size, (ncclWindow_t*)&handle, NCCL_WIN_COLL_SYMMETRIC),\n        c10::str(\n            \"Failed to window register segment with ptr \",\n            ptr,\n            \", size \",\n            size,\n            \" on ncclComm_ \",\n            comm));\n  } else {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n        ncclCommRegister(comm, ptr, size, &handle),\n        c10::str(\n            \"Failed to register segment with ptr \",\n            ptr,\n            \", size \",\n            size,\n            \" on ncclComm_ \",\n            comm));\n  }\n#else\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n      ncclCommRegister(comm, ptr, size, &handle),\n      c10::str(\n          \"Failed to register segment with ptr \",\n          ptr,\n          \", size \",\n          size,\n          \" on ncclComm_ \",\n          comm));\n#endif\n  registeredSegmentHandles_[ptr] = handle;\n  return ncclSuccess;\n#else\n  return ncclInvalidUsage;\n#endif\n}\n\nncclResult_t NCCLComm::deregisterSegment(void* ptr, bool window /*false*/) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_COMM_REGISTER\n  TORCH_CHECK(\n      registeredSegmentHandles_.count(ptr) == 1,\n      \"Segment with ptr \",\n      ptr,\n      \" is not registered on ncclComm_ \",\n      ncclComm_);\n\n  void* handle = registeredSegmentHandles_[ptr];\n  // Use getNcclComm to make sure comm is ready before calling nccl APIs\n  auto comm = getNcclComm();\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_COMM_WINDOW_REGISTER\n  if (window) {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n        ncclCommWindowDeregister(comm, (ncclWindow_t)handle),\n        c10::str(\n            \"Failed to window deregister segment handle \",\n            handle,\n            \", with ptr \",\n            ptr,\n            \" on ncclComm_ \",\n            comm));\n  } else {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n        ncclCommDeregister(comm, handle),\n        c10::str(\n            \"Failed to deregister segment handle \",\n            handle,\n            \", with ptr \",\n            ptr,\n            \" on ncclComm_ \",\n            comm));\n  }\n#else\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n      ncclCommDeregister(comm, handle),\n      c10::str(\n          \"Failed to deregister segment handle \",\n          handle,\n          \", with ptr \",\n          ptr,\n          \" on ncclComm_ \",\n          comm));\n#endif\n  registeredSegmentHandles_.erase(ptr);\n  return ncclSuccess;\n#else\n  return ncclInvalidUsage;\n#endif\n}\n\nstd::string NCCLComm::repr() const {\n  return c10::str((void*)ncclComm_);\n}\n\n#if (defined(IS_NCCLX) || defined(USE_ROCM)) && defined(NCCL_COMM_DUMP)\nstd::unordered_map<std::string, std::string> NCCLComm::ncclCommDump() {\n  std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string> dump;\n  if (isAborted()) {\n    LOG(INFO) << \"Communicator was aborted before trying to dump its state.\";\n    return dump;\n  }\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(::ncclCommDump(ncclComm_, dump), std::nullopt);\n  return dump;\n}\n#endif\n\nstd::string getNcclVersion() {\n  static std::string versionString = []() {\n    auto [ncclMajor, ncclMinor, ncclPatch] = getNcclVersionTuple();\n    std::string versionString;\n    if (ncclMajor == 0 && ncclMinor == 0 && ncclPatch == 0) {\n      versionString = \"Unknown NCCL version\";\n    } else {\n      versionString = std::to_string(ncclMajor) + \".\" +\n          std::to_string(ncclMinor) + \".\" + std::to_string(ncclPatch);\n#ifdef NCCL_SUFFIX\n      const auto ncclSuffix = std::string(NCCL_SUFFIX);\n      if (!ncclSuffix.empty()) {\n        versionString += \".\" + ncclSuffix;\n      }\n#endif\n    }\n    return versionString;\n  }();\n\n  return versionString;\n}\n\nstd::tuple<int, int, int> getNcclVersionTuple() {\n  static std::tuple<int, int, int> versionTuple = []() {\n    int version = getNcclVersionNumber();\n    // can't compute the version if call did not return successfully or version\n    // code < 100 (corresponding to 0.1.0)\n    if (version < 100) {\n      return std::make_tuple(0, 0, 0);\n    }\n    // NCCL changed version coding starting 2.9\n    const int majorBase = version < 2900 ? 1000 : 10000;\n    const int minorBase = 100;\n    auto ncclMajor = version / majorBase;\n    auto ncclMinor = (version % majorBase) / minorBase;\n    auto ncclPatch = version % minorBase;\n    return std::make_tuple(ncclMajor, ncclMinor, ncclPatch);\n  }();\n  return versionTuple;\n}\n\nint getNcclVersionNumber() {\n  static int version = []() {\n    int version = 0;\n    ncclResult_t status = ncclGetVersion(&version);\n    if (status != ncclSuccess) {\n      return 0; // Error.\n    }\n    return version;\n  }();\n  return version;\n}\n\nsize_t hashTensors(const std::vector<at::Tensor>& tensors) {\n  size_t hash = 0;\n  for (auto& tensor : tensors) {\n    if (tensor.numel() > 0 && tensor.storage()) {\n      size_t data_size = tensor.storage().nbytes();\n      if (data_size > 0 && tensor.storage().data_ptr()) {\n        auto src = static_cast<const char*>(tensor.storage().data_ptr().get());\n        std::vector<char> dst(data_size);\n        // This is needed so that we trigger a device synchronization so we can\n        // get the collective finished if launched on GPU and hash its output.\n        AT_CUDA_CHECK(\n            cudaMemcpy(dst.data(), src, data_size, cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost));\n        for (size_t i = 0; i < data_size; ++i) {\n          // Update the hash for each byte in the tensor\n          hash = c10::hash_combine(hash, c10::get_hash(dst[i], data_size));\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return hash;\n}\n\n// NCCL uses Non-negative int to represent in-group according to API\n// requirement. We take a list of ranks and generate a hash value based on the\n// list and ensure its range of 32-bit int.\nint genNcclSplitColor(const std::vector<int>& ranks) {\n  // Combine the hash values using a simple reducer (std::hash + fold)\n  std::size_t combined_hash = std::accumulate(\n      ranks.begin(),\n      ranks.end(),\n      std::size_t(0),\n      [](std::size_t acc, int rank) {\n        return acc ^\n            (std::hash<int>{}(rank) + 0x9e3779b9 + (acc << 6) + (acc >> 2));\n      });\n\n  // max positive value of int32_t\n  constexpr int32_t max_c_int = std::numeric_limits<int32_t>::max();\n  int color = static_cast<int>(\n      std::abs(static_cast<int64_t>(combined_hash)) % max_c_int);\n  return color;\n}\n\n// Default value: 30 minutes\nint nccl_nonblocking_timeout() {\n  static int timeout = -2; // -2 means not initialized\n  if (timeout == -2) {\n    const auto val = c10::utils::get_env(\"TORCH_NCCL_NONBLOCKING_TIMEOUT\");\n    if (val.has_value() && !val.value().empty()) {\n      timeout = stoi(val.value());\n    } else {\n      // Default value consistent with kBackendDefaultTimeout\n      timeout = 30 * 60;\n    }\n  }\n  return timeout;\n}\n\nstd::string ncclGetErrorWithVersion(ncclResult_t error) {\n  return std::string(ncclGetErrorString(error)) + \", NCCL version \" +\n      getNcclVersion();\n}\n\n// Provides additional detail into NCCL error codes based on when these are\n// thrown in the NCCL codebase.\nstd::string getNcclErrorDetailStr(\n    ncclResult_t error,\n    std::optional<std::string> processGroupFailureReason /* = std::nullopt */\n) {\n  // Prioritize failure reason provided by PG NCCL first, as it can abort\n  // communicators when it encounters collective timeouts, etc.\n  if (processGroupFailureReason != std::nullopt) {\n    return *processGroupFailureReason;\n  }\n  std::string interpret;\n  std::string err;\n#ifdef ENABLE_NCCL_GET_LAST_ERROR\n  auto ret = ncclGetLastError(nullptr);\n  if (ret) {\n    err = \"\\nLast error:\\n\" + std::string(ret);\n  } else {\n    err = \"\\nLast error: Unknown NCCL Error\\n\";\n  }\n#endif\n  switch (error) {\n    case ncclUnhandledCudaError:\n      interpret = \"ncclUnhandledCudaError: Call to CUDA function failed.\";\n      break;\n    case ncclSystemError:\n      interpret =\n          \"ncclSystemError: System call (e.g. socket, malloc) or external library call failed or device error. \";\n#ifndef NCCL_REMOTE_ERROR\n      // Before ncclRemoteError was created, unexpected remote disconnect was\n      // categorized as ncclSystemError\n      interpret += \"It can be also caused by unexpected exit of a remote peer.\";\n#endif\n      break;\n    case ncclInternalError:\n      interpret = \"ncclInternalError: Internal check failed.\";\n      break;\n    case ncclInvalidArgument:\n      interpret = \"ncclInvalidArgument: Invalid value for an argument.\";\n      break;\n    case ncclInvalidUsage:\n      interpret =\n          \"ncclInvalidUsage: This usually reflects invalid usage of NCCL library.\";\n      break;\n#ifdef NCCL_REMOTE_ERROR\n    case ncclRemoteError:\n      interpret =\n          \"ncclRemoteError: A call failed possibly due to a network error or a remote process exiting prematurely.\";\n      break;\n#endif\n    default:\n      interpret = \"Unknown NCCL error!\";\n  }\n  return interpret + err;\n}\n\n// Helper function that gets the data type and issues error if not supported\nncclDataType_t getNcclDataType(at::ScalarType type) {\n  auto it = ncclDataType.find(type);\n  TORCH_CHECK_WITH(\n      TypeError,\n      it != ncclDataType.end(),\n      \"Input tensor data type is not supported for NCCL process group: \",\n      type);\n  return it->second;\n}\n\n// Dump proxyTrace log to stdout\nvoid printNcclCommProxyTrace(\n    const std::string& dumpReason,\n    const std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string>& dumpMap) {\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Dumping nccl comm trace, reason: \" << dumpReason;\n  for (auto& [key, value] : dumpMap) {\n    LOG(INFO) << \"key: \" << key << \", value: \" << value;\n  }\n  LOG(INFO) << \"----------------------\";\n}\n\n} // namespace c10d\n\n#endif // USE_C10D_NCCL\n",

    "content": "}\n\nvoid NCCLComm::destroy() {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (aborted_) {\n    LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << rank_\n              << \": NCCL communicator already Invalidated. Skip destroy.\";\n    return;\n  }\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(deviceIndex_);\n  auto comm = getNcclComm();\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(ncclCommDestroy(comm), std::nullopt);\n  // Poison future getNcclComm\n  aborted_ = true;\n}\n\nvoid NCCLComm::abort(std::optional<std::string> commFailureReason) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(deviceIndex_);\n#ifdef ENABLE_NCCL_ERROR_CHECKING\n  if (aborted_ && !initialized_) {\n    // Should not abort twice.\n    return;\n  }\n\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_COMM_REGISTER\n  // Deregister all registered segments before aborting.\n  for (auto& it : registeredSegmentHandles_) {\n    void* handle = it.second;\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n        ::ncclCommDeregister(ncclComm_, handle),\n        c10::str(\n            \"Failed to deregister segment handle \",\n            handle,\n            \" on ncclComm_ \",\n            ncclComm_));\n  }\n  registeredSegmentHandles_.clear();\n#endif\n\n  // Set true failure reason if provided by ProcessGroupNCCL (e.g. work\n  // timeout)\n  commFailureReason_ = commFailureReason;\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Aborting ncclComm_ \" << ncclComm_ << \" with reason: \"\n            << (commFailureReason ? *commFailureReason\n                                  : \"No abort reason provided.\");\n#ifndef NCCL_HAS_COMM_NONBLOCKING\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(::ncclCommAbort(ncclComm_), commFailureReason_);\n#else\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_TIMEOUT(\n      ::ncclCommAbort(ncclComm_), ncclComm_, commFailureReason_);\n#endif\n  aborted_ = true;\n  ncclComm_ = nullptr;\n\n  // Set an appropriate error so that we avoid using the communicator.\n  if (ncclAsyncErr_ == ncclSuccess) {\n    ncclAsyncErr_ = ncclSystemError;\n  }\n#else",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/NCCLUtils.cpp",

{
  "filePath": "/workspace/torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/NCCLUtils.cpp",
  "newString": "  // Set true failure reason if provided by ProcessGroupNCCL (e.g. work\n  // timeout)\n  commFailureReason_ = commFailureReason;\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Aborting ncclComm_ \" << ncclComm_ << \" with reason: \"\n            << (commFailureReason ? *commFailureReason\n                                  : \"No abort reason provided.\");\n#ifndef NCCL_HAS_COMM_NONBLOCKING\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(::ncclCommAbort(ncclComm_), commFailureReason_);\n#else\n  // Inline polling with mutex already held.\n  // We already hold the mutex_ lock here, so the direct call to\n  // ncclCommGetAsyncError is safe.\n  {\n    ncclResult_t result = ::ncclCommAbort(ncclComm_);\n    auto startTimepoint = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();\n    auto timeout = nccl_nonblocking_timeout();\n    while (result == ncclInProgress) {\n      C10D_CHECK_TIMEOUT(startTimepoint, timeout);\n      sched_yield();\n      ncclCommGetAsyncError(ncclComm_, &result);\n    }\n    if (result != ncclSuccess) {\n      std::string err = \"NCCL error in: \" + std::string(__FILE__) + \":\" +\n          std::to_string(__LINE__) + \", \" + ncclGetErrorWithVersion(result) +\n          \"\\n\" + getNcclErrorDetailStr(result, commFailureReason_);\n      TORCH_CHECK_WITH(DistBackendError, false, err);\n    }\n  }\n#endif\n  aborted_ = true;",
  "oldString": "  // Set true failure reason if provided by ProcessGroupNCCL (e.g. work\n  // timeout)\n  commFailureReason_ = commFailureReason;\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Aborting ncclComm_ \" << ncclComm_ << \" with reason: \"\n            << (commFailureReason ? *commFailureReason\n                                  : \"No abort reason provided.\");\n#ifndef NCCL_HAS_COMM_NONBLOCKING\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(::ncclCommAbort(ncclComm_), commFailureReason_);\n#else\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_TIMEOUT(\n      ::ncclCommAbort(ncclComm_), ncclComm_, commFailureReason_);\n#endif\n  aborted_ = true;",
  "originalFile": "#include <torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/NCCLUtils.hpp>\n\n#ifdef USE_C10D_NCCL\n#include <fmt/format.h>\n#include <mutex>\n#include <thread>\n#include <vector>\n\nnamespace c10d {\n\nNCCLComm::NCCLComm(ncclComm_t ncclComm) : ncclComm_(ncclComm) {}\n\nNCCLComm::~NCCLComm() noexcept {\n  // (kwen2501) Making CUDA/NCCL calls in this destructor can hit CUDA driver\n  // shutdown error if CUDA context has exited first. Thus, we are not\n  // destroying or aborting NCCL communicators here. We just detect and warn\n  // about the risk of memory leak. Normally, a user would have called\n  // `destroy_process_group` or `abort_process_group`, and such risk would be\n  // avoided.\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (ncclComm_ && initialized_ && !aborted_) {\n    TORCH_WARN_ONCE(\n        \"WARNING: NCCL communicator hasn't been destroyed. This may cause \"\n        \"memory leaks. To avoid the risk, you can call `destroy_process_group` \"\n        \"during normal exit or `_abort_process_group` when handling failures.\")\n  }\n}\n\n// NOLINTNEXTLINE(*-noexcept-move-*)\nNCCLComm::NCCLComm(NCCLComm&& other) {\n  // Using other's lock, as it reads other's states\n  // Can not use this.mutex_, as this object is being constructed.\n  LockType lock(other.mutex_);\n  std::swap(ncclComm_, other.ncclComm_);\n  std::swap(aborted_, other.aborted_);\n  std::swap(ncclAsyncErr_, other.ncclAsyncErr_);\n  std::swap(initialized_, other.initialized_);\n  std::swap(nonBlocking_, other.nonBlocking_);\n  std::swap(deviceIndex_, other.deviceIndex_);\n}\n\nvoid NCCLComm::setUniqueHash(ncclUniqueId ncclId) {\n  const uint8_t* bytes = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(&ncclId);\n\n  fmt::memory_buffer buf;\n  buf.reserve(NCCL_UNIQUE_ID_BYTES * 2); // 2 hex chars per byte\n  for (int i = 0; i < NCCL_UNIQUE_ID_BYTES; ++i) {\n    fmt::format_to(\n        std::back_inserter(buf), \"{:02x}\", static_cast<int>(bytes[i]));\n  }\n  this->uniqueHash_ = fmt::to_string(buf);\n}\n\nvoid NCCLComm::setUniqueHash(std::string hash) {\n  this->uniqueHash_ = std::move(hash);\n}\n\nstd::string NCCLComm::getUniqueHash() {\n  return uniqueHash_;\n}\n\nstd::shared_ptr<NCCLComm> NCCLComm::create(\n    int numRanks,\n    int rank,\n    ncclUniqueId commId,\n    at::DeviceIndex deviceIndex) {\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(deviceIndex);\n  auto comm = std::make_shared<NCCLComm>();\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n      ncclCommInitRank(&(comm->ncclComm_), numRanks, commId, rank),\n      std::nullopt);\n  comm->setUniqueHash(commId);\n  comm->rank_ = rank;\n  comm->deviceIndex_ = deviceIndex;\n  comm->initialized_ = true;\n  // Old style comm is always blocking.\n  comm->nonBlocking_ = false;\n  return comm;\n}\n\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_CONFIG\nstd::shared_ptr<NCCLComm> NCCLComm::create(\n    int numRanks,\n    int rank,\n    ncclUniqueId commId,\n    at::DeviceIndex deviceIndex,\n    ncclConfig_t& config) {\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(deviceIndex);\n  auto comm = std::make_shared<NCCLComm>();\n  comm->nonBlocking_ = config.blocking == 0;\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << rank << \": creating NCCL communicator with mode: \"\n            << (comm->nonBlocking_ ? \"nonblocking\" : \"blocking\");\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_NONBLOCKING(\n      ncclCommInitRankConfig(\n          &(comm->ncclComm_), numRanks, commId, rank, &config),\n      std::nullopt);\n  comm->setUniqueHash(commId);\n  comm->rank_ = rank;\n  comm->deviceIndex_ = deviceIndex;\n  // Under blocking mode, comm is initialized immediately after NCCL init\n  // returns; Under nonblocking mode, we check whether comm is initialized the\n  // *next* time ncclComm_ is accessed.\n  comm->initialized_ = !comm->nonBlocking_;\n  return comm;\n}\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_INIT_RANK_SCALABLE\nstd::shared_ptr<NCCLComm> NCCLComm::create_scalable(\n    int numRanks,\n    int rank,\n    std::vector<ncclUniqueId>& commIds,\n    at::DeviceIndex deviceIndex,\n    ncclConfig_t& config) {\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(deviceIndex);\n  auto comm = std::make_shared<NCCLComm>();\n  comm->nonBlocking_ = config.blocking == 0;\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << rank << \": creating NCCL communicator with mode: \"\n            << (comm->nonBlocking_ ? \"nonblocking\" : \"blocking\")\n            << \" with scalable init.\";\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_NONBLOCKING(\n      ncclCommInitRankScalable(\n          &(comm->ncclComm_),\n          numRanks,\n          rank,\n          commIds.size(),\n          commIds.data(),\n          &config),\n      std::nullopt);\n  // Only the first ncclUniqueId will be used to create the\n  // communicator hash id, which is used to identify the communicator\n  // in the log file and in the replay tool.\n  comm->setUniqueHash(commIds[0]);\n  comm->rank_ = rank;\n  comm->deviceIndex_ = deviceIndex;\n  comm->initialized_ = !comm->nonBlocking_;\n  return comm;\n}\n#endif // NCCL_HAS_INIT_RANK_SCALABLE\n#endif // NCCL_HAS_CONFIG\n\nncclComm_t NCCLComm::getNcclComm() {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (aborted_) {\n    auto commFailureMsg = commFailureReason_ != std::nullopt\n        ? c10::str(\" Original reason for failure was: \", *commFailureReason_)\n        : \"\";\n    TORCH_CHECK_WITH(\n        DistBackendError,\n        false,\n        c10::str(\n            \"NCCL communicator was aborted on rank \",\n            rank_,\n            \". \",\n            commFailureMsg));\n  }\n  // In non-blocking mode, ensure comm is ready.\n  if (nonBlocking_) {\n    // Wait with long interval if communicator is being initialized.\n    bool longInterval = !initialized_;\n    waitReady(longInterval);\n    // ncclComm_ should be initialized by now\n  }\n  if (!initialized_) {\n    // TODO: see if we can consolidate other `initialized_` flipping here.\n    // Maintaining it elsewhere is some work.\n    initialized_ = true;\n    LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << rank_ << \": NCCL communicator \" << repr()\n              << \" is initialized.\";\n  }\n  return ncclComm_;\n}\n\nat::DeviceIndex NCCLComm::getDeviceIndex() {\n  return deviceIndex_;\n}\n\n// Wait for the communicator to be ready. This is a blocking function.\n// Arguments:\n//   longInterval: if true, wait with sleep of an interval; otherwise, wait\n//   with `sched_yield` which is faster (but acquires CPU more frequently).\nvoid NCCLComm::waitReady(bool longInterval) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (aborted_)\n    return;\n  // Inline polling with mutex already held.\n  // We already hold the mutex_ lock here, so the direct call to\n  // ncclCommGetAsyncError is safe.\n  ncclResult_t result = ncclInProgress;\n  auto startTimepoint = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();\n  auto timeout = nccl_nonblocking_timeout();\n  while (result == ncclInProgress) {\n    C10D_CHECK_TIMEOUT(startTimepoint, timeout);\n    if (longInterval) {\n      C10D_SCHED_SLEEP();\n    } else {\n      sched_yield();\n    }\n    ncclCommGetAsyncError(ncclComm_, &result);\n  }\n  if (result != ncclSuccess) {\n    std::string err = \"NCCL error in: \" + std::string(__FILE__) + \":\" +\n        std::to_string(__LINE__) + \", \" + ncclGetErrorWithVersion(result) +\n        \"\\n\" + getNcclErrorDetailStr(result, std::nullopt);\n    TORCH_CHECK_WITH(DistBackendError, false, err);\n  }\n}\n\nstd::optional<std::string> NCCLComm::getNcclCommFailureReason() const {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  return commFailureReason_;\n}\n\n#if defined(NCCL_HAS_COMM_SPLIT)\nstd::shared_ptr<NCCLComm> NCCLComm::split(\n    NCCLComm* source,\n    int color_id,\n    int rank,\n    ncclConfig_t& config) {\n  TORCH_CHECK(\n      color_id >= NCCL_SPLIT_NOCOLOR,\n      \"Color must be a non-negative value or NCCL_SPLIT_NOCOLOR (-1)\"\n      \", but got \",\n      color_id);\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << source->rank_ << \": split from parent comm \"\n            << source->repr() << \" with color_id \" << color_id << \" and rank \"\n            << rank;\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(source->deviceIndex_);\n  auto comm = std::make_shared<NCCLComm>();\n  // This call will block until the source communicator is initialized\n  auto sourceComm = source->getNcclComm();\n#ifndef NCCL_HAS_COMM_NONBLOCKING\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n      ncclCommSplit(sourceComm, color_id, rank, &(comm->ncclComm_), &config),\n      std::nullopt);\n#else\n  // After calling ncclCommSplit in non-blocking mode, we should wait for the\n  // source communicator to be out of ncclInProgress state.\n  // Reason 1:\n  //   it's unsafe to call new operations on the parent comm while it's in\n  //   ncclInProgress state.\n  // Reason 2:\n  //   as of NCCL 2.23, the ptr value of child comm will not be filled until the\n  //   state of parent comm is ncclSuccess. This may change in the future. See:\n  //   https://github.com/NVIDIA/nccl/issues/1472\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_TIMEOUT_SLEEP(\n      ncclCommSplit(sourceComm, color_id, rank, &(comm->ncclComm_), &config),\n      sourceComm, // wait on parent comm\n      std::nullopt);\n  if (color_id >= 0) {\n    // Waiting for parent comm above still does not seem to guarantee the child\n    // comm ptr is valid. Therefore we add a manual wait here for safety.\n    // TODO: remove this wait after NCCL fix the semantics.\n    auto startTime = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();\n    auto timeout = nccl_nonblocking_timeout();\n    while (!comm->ncclComm_) {\n      C10D_CHECK_TIMEOUT(startTime, timeout);\n      C10D_SCHED_SLEEP();\n    }\n  }\n  // comm->ncclComm_ should have valid ptr by now, but not necessarily\n  // initialized. Rely on getNcclComm() to wait for its initialization.\n#endif\n  ++source->ncclCommSplitCounter_;\n  comm->rank_ = rank;\n  // Child comm should be on the same device as parent comm\n  comm->deviceIndex_ = source->deviceIndex_;\n  comm->nonBlocking_ = config.blocking == 0;\n  comm->setUniqueHash(\n      source->getUniqueHash() + \":\" +\n      std::to_string(source->ncclCommSplitCounter_));\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << source->rank_ << \": created child comm \"\n            << comm->repr() << \" with color_id \" << color_id;\n  return comm;\n}\n#endif\n\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_COMM_SHRINK\nstd::shared_ptr<NCCLComm> NCCLComm::shrink(\n    NCCLComm* source,\n    std::vector<int>& ranks_to_exclude,\n    ncclConfig_t* config,\n    int shrinkFlags) {\n  // Preconditions are validated in ProcessGroupNCCL::shrink\n\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << source->rank_ << \": shrinking comm \" << source->repr()\n            << \" excluding \" << ranks_to_exclude.size() << \" ranks\";\n\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(source->deviceIndex_);\n  auto comm = std::make_shared<NCCLComm>();\n\n  // This call will block until the source communicator is initialized\n  auto sourceComm = source->getNcclComm();\n\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_NONBLOCKING(\n      ncclCommShrink(\n          sourceComm,\n          ranks_to_exclude.data(),\n          ranks_to_exclude.size(),\n          reinterpret_cast<ncclComm_t*>(&(comm->ncclComm_)),\n          config,\n          shrinkFlags),\n      source->getNcclCommFailureReason());\n\n  // Wait for the child communicator to be ready\n  source->waitReady(true);\n  comm->initialized_ = true;\n\n  // NCCL automatically assigns rank during shrink - query it efficiently\n  int assigned_rank;\n  try {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n        ncclCommUserRank(comm->ncclComm_, &assigned_rank), std::nullopt);\n    comm->rank_ = assigned_rank;\n  } catch (const std::exception& e) {\n    // Fallback: if ncclCommUserRank fails, we can't determine the rank\n    LOG(ERROR) << \"Failed to query NCCL-assigned rank: \" << e.what();\n    throw;\n  }\n\n  // Child comm should be on the same device as parent comm\n  comm->deviceIndex_ = source->deviceIndex_;\n  if (config != nullptr) {\n    comm->nonBlocking_ = config->blocking == 0;\n  } else {\n    // Inherit parent behavior if no config provided\n    comm->nonBlocking_ = source->nonBlocking_;\n  }\n\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << source->rank_ << \": created shrunken comm \"\n            << comm->repr() << \" with NCCL-assigned rank \" << assigned_rank;\n\n  return comm;\n}\n#endif\n\nvoid NCCLComm::finalize() {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (aborted_) {\n    LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << rank_\n              << \": NCCL communicator already Invalidated. Skip finalize.\";\n    return;\n  }\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(deviceIndex_);\n  auto comm = getNcclComm();\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_NONBLOCKING(ncclCommFinalize(comm), std::nullopt);\n}\n\nvoid NCCLComm::destroy() {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  if (aborted_) {\n    LOG(INFO) << \"Rank \" << rank_\n              << \": NCCL communicator already Invalidated. Skip destroy.\";\n    return;\n  }\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(deviceIndex_);\n  auto comm = getNcclComm();\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(ncclCommDestroy(comm), std::nullopt);\n  // Poison future getNcclComm\n  aborted_ = true;\n}\n\nvoid NCCLComm::abort(std::optional<std::string> commFailureReason) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  at::cuda::OptionalCUDAGuard gpuGuard(deviceIndex_);\n#ifdef ENABLE_NCCL_ERROR_CHECKING\n  if (aborted_ && !initialized_) {\n    // Should not abort twice.\n    return;\n  }\n\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_COMM_REGISTER\n  // Deregister all registered segments before aborting.\n  for (auto& it : registeredSegmentHandles_) {\n    void* handle = it.second;\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n        ::ncclCommDeregister(ncclComm_, handle),\n        c10::str(\n            \"Failed to deregister segment handle \",\n            handle,\n            \" on ncclComm_ \",\n            ncclComm_));\n  }\n  registeredSegmentHandles_.clear();\n#endif\n\n  // Set true failure reason if provided by ProcessGroupNCCL (e.g. work\n  // timeout)\n  commFailureReason_ = commFailureReason;\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Aborting ncclComm_ \" << ncclComm_ << \" with reason: \"\n            << (commFailureReason ? *commFailureReason\n                                  : \"No abort reason provided.\");\n#ifndef NCCL_HAS_COMM_NONBLOCKING\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(::ncclCommAbort(ncclComm_), commFailureReason_);\n#else\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_TIMEOUT(\n      ::ncclCommAbort(ncclComm_), ncclComm_, commFailureReason_);\n#endif\n  aborted_ = true;\n  ncclComm_ = nullptr;\n\n  // Set an appropriate error so that we avoid using the communicator.\n  if (ncclAsyncErr_ == ncclSuccess) {\n    ncclAsyncErr_ = ncclSystemError;\n  }\n#else\n  // This is a NOOP, if error checks are disabled.\n  return;\n#endif\n}\n\nbool NCCLComm::isInitialized() const {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  return initialized_;\n}\n\nbool NCCLComm::isAborted() const {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  return aborted_;\n}\n\nuint64_t NCCLComm::getCommSplitCounter() const {\n  return ncclCommSplitCounter_;\n}\n\nncclResult_t NCCLComm::checkForNcclError() {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n#ifdef ENABLE_NCCL_ERROR_CHECKING\n  if (ncclAsyncErr_ != ncclSuccess) {\n    return ncclAsyncErr_;\n  }\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n      ncclCommGetAsyncError(ncclComm_, &ncclAsyncErr_), commFailureReason_);\n  return ncclAsyncErr_;\n#else\n  // Always return success, if error checks are disabled.\n  return ncclSuccess;\n#endif\n}\n\nncclResult_t NCCLComm::getAsyncError(ncclResult_t* asyncError) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n  return ncclCommGetAsyncError(ncclComm_, asyncError);\n}\n\nncclResult_t NCCLComm::registerSegment(\n    void* ptr,\n    size_t size,\n    bool errorOnRereg, /*=true*/\n    bool window /*=false*/) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_COMM_REGISTER\n  // We register only segments from cache allocator\n  // which are guaranteed to be with disjoint addr ranges. Thus, a ptr always\n  // maps to a unique handle and should not be registered before the current\n  // ptr is deregistered and freed.\n  if (registeredSegmentHandles_.count(ptr) > 0) {\n    TORCH_CHECK(\n        !errorOnRereg,\n        \"Segment with ptr \",\n        ptr,\n        \" has already been registered on ncclComm_ \",\n        ncclComm_);\n    // Skip below\n    return ncclSuccess;\n  }\n\n  void* handle = nullptr;\n  // Use getNcclComm to make sure comm is ready before calling nccl APIs\n  auto comm = getNcclComm();\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_COMM_WINDOW_REGISTER\n  if (window) {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n        ncclCommWindowRegister(\n            comm, ptr, size, (ncclWindow_t*)&handle, NCCL_WIN_COLL_SYMMETRIC),\n        c10::str(\n            \"Failed to window register segment with ptr \",\n            ptr,\n            \", size \",\n            size,\n            \" on ncclComm_ \",\n            comm));\n  } else {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n        ncclCommRegister(comm, ptr, size, &handle),\n        c10::str(\n            \"Failed to register segment with ptr \",\n            ptr,\n            \", size \",\n            size,\n            \" on ncclComm_ \",\n            comm));\n  }\n#else\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n      ncclCommRegister(comm, ptr, size, &handle),\n      c10::str(\n          \"Failed to register segment with ptr \",\n          ptr,\n          \", size \",\n          size,\n          \" on ncclComm_ \",\n          comm));\n#endif\n  registeredSegmentHandles_[ptr] = handle;\n  return ncclSuccess;\n#else\n  return ncclInvalidUsage;\n#endif\n}\n\nncclResult_t NCCLComm::deregisterSegment(void* ptr, bool window /*false*/) {\n  LockType lock(mutex_);\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_COMM_REGISTER\n  TORCH_CHECK(\n      registeredSegmentHandles_.count(ptr) == 1,\n      \"Segment with ptr \",\n      ptr,\n      \" is not registered on ncclComm_ \",\n      ncclComm_);\n\n  void* handle = registeredSegmentHandles_[ptr];\n  // Use getNcclComm to make sure comm is ready before calling nccl APIs\n  auto comm = getNcclComm();\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_COMM_WINDOW_REGISTER\n  if (window) {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n        ncclCommWindowDeregister(comm, (ncclWindow_t)handle),\n        c10::str(\n            \"Failed to window deregister segment handle \",\n            handle,\n            \", with ptr \",\n            ptr,\n            \" on ncclComm_ \",\n            comm));\n  } else {\n    C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n        ncclCommDeregister(comm, handle),\n        c10::str(\n            \"Failed to deregister segment handle \",\n            handle,\n            \", with ptr \",\n            ptr,\n            \" on ncclComm_ \",\n            comm));\n  }\n#else\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n      ncclCommDeregister(comm, handle),\n      c10::str(\n          \"Failed to deregister segment handle \",\n          handle,\n          \", with ptr \",\n          ptr,\n          \" on ncclComm_ \",\n          comm));\n#endif\n  registeredSegmentHandles_.erase(ptr);\n  return ncclSuccess;\n#else\n  return ncclInvalidUsage;\n#endif\n}\n\nstd::string NCCLComm::repr() const {\n  return c10::str((void*)ncclComm_);\n}\n\n#if (defined(IS_NCCLX) || defined(USE_ROCM)) && defined(NCCL_COMM_DUMP)\nstd::unordered_map<std::string, std::string> NCCLComm::ncclCommDump() {\n  std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string> dump;\n  if (isAborted()) {\n    LOG(INFO) << \"Communicator was aborted before trying to dump its state.\";\n    return dump;\n  }\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(::ncclCommDump(ncclComm_, dump), std::nullopt);\n  return dump;\n}\n#endif\n\nstd::string getNcclVersion() {\n  static std::string versionString = []() {\n    auto [ncclMajor, ncclMinor, ncclPatch] = getNcclVersionTuple();\n    std::string versionString;\n    if (ncclMajor == 0 && ncclMinor == 0 && ncclPatch == 0) {\n      versionString = \"Unknown NCCL version\";\n    } else {\n      versionString = std::to_string(ncclMajor) + \".\" +\n          std::to_string(ncclMinor) + \".\" + std::to_string(ncclPatch);\n#ifdef NCCL_SUFFIX\n      const auto ncclSuffix = std::string(NCCL_SUFFIX);\n      if (!ncclSuffix.empty()) {\n        versionString += \".\" + ncclSuffix;\n      }\n#endif\n    }\n    return versionString;\n  }();\n\n  return versionString;\n}\n\nstd::tuple<int, int, int> getNcclVersionTuple() {\n  static std::tuple<int, int, int> versionTuple = []() {\n    int version = getNcclVersionNumber();\n    // can't compute the version if call did not return successfully or version\n    // code < 100 (corresponding to 0.1.0)\n    if (version < 100) {\n      return std::make_tuple(0, 0, 0);\n    }\n    // NCCL changed version coding starting 2.9\n    const int majorBase = version < 2900 ? 1000 : 10000;\n    const int minorBase = 100;\n    auto ncclMajor = version / majorBase;\n    auto ncclMinor = (version % majorBase) / minorBase;\n    auto ncclPatch = version % minorBase;\n    return std::make_tuple(ncclMajor, ncclMinor, ncclPatch);\n  }();\n  return versionTuple;\n}\n\nint getNcclVersionNumber() {\n  static int version = []() {\n    int version = 0;\n    ncclResult_t status = ncclGetVersion(&version);\n    if (status != ncclSuccess) {\n      return 0; // Error.\n    }\n    return version;\n  }();\n  return version;\n}\n\nsize_t hashTensors(const std::vector<at::Tensor>& tensors) {\n  size_t hash = 0;\n  for (auto& tensor : tensors) {\n    if (tensor.numel() > 0 && tensor.storage()) {\n      size_t data_size = tensor.storage().nbytes();\n      if (data_size > 0 && tensor.storage().data_ptr()) {\n        auto src = static_cast<const char*>(tensor.storage().data_ptr().get());\n        std::vector<char> dst(data_size);\n        // This is needed so that we trigger a device synchronization so we can\n        // get the collective finished if launched on GPU and hash its output.\n        AT_CUDA_CHECK(\n            cudaMemcpy(dst.data(), src, data_size, cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost));\n        for (size_t i = 0; i < data_size; ++i) {\n          // Update the hash for each byte in the tensor\n          hash = c10::hash_combine(hash, c10::get_hash(dst[i], data_size));\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return hash;\n}\n\n// NCCL uses Non-negative int to represent in-group according to API\n// requirement. We take a list of ranks and generate a hash value based on the\n// list and ensure its range of 32-bit int.\nint genNcclSplitColor(const std::vector<int>& ranks) {\n  // Combine the hash values using a simple reducer (std::hash + fold)\n  std::size_t combined_hash = std::accumulate(\n      ranks.begin(),\n      ranks.end(),\n      std::size_t(0),\n      [](std::size_t acc, int rank) {\n        return acc ^\n            (std::hash<int>{}(rank) + 0x9e3779b9 + (acc << 6) + (acc >> 2));\n      });\n\n  // max positive value of int32_t\n  constexpr int32_t max_c_int = std::numeric_limits<int32_t>::max();\n  int color = static_cast<int>(\n      std::abs(static_cast<int64_t>(combined_hash)) % max_c_int);\n  return color;\n}\n\n// Default value: 30 minutes\nint nccl_nonblocking_timeout() {\n  static int timeout = -2; // -2 means not initialized\n  if (timeout == -2) {\n    const auto val = c10::utils::get_env(\"TORCH_NCCL_NONBLOCKING_TIMEOUT\");\n    if (val.has_value() && !val.value().empty()) {\n      timeout = stoi(val.value());\n    } else {\n      // Default value consistent with kBackendDefaultTimeout\n      timeout = 30 * 60;\n    }\n  }\n  return timeout;\n}\n\nstd::string ncclGetErrorWithVersion(ncclResult_t error) {\n  return std::string(ncclGetErrorString(error)) + \", NCCL version \" +\n      getNcclVersion();\n}\n\n// Provides additional detail into NCCL error codes based on when these are\n// thrown in the NCCL codebase.\nstd::string getNcclErrorDetailStr(\n    ncclResult_t error,\n    std::optional<std::string> processGroupFailureReason /* = std::nullopt */\n) {\n  // Prioritize failure reason provided by PG NCCL first, as it can abort\n  // communicators when it encounters collective timeouts, etc.\n  if (processGroupFailureReason != std::nullopt) {\n    return *processGroupFailureReason;\n  }\n  std::string interpret;\n  std::string err;\n#ifdef ENABLE_NCCL_GET_LAST_ERROR\n  auto ret = ncclGetLastError(nullptr);\n  if (ret) {\n    err = \"\\nLast error:\\n\" + std::string(ret);\n  } else {\n    err = \"\\nLast error: Unknown NCCL Error\\n\";\n  }\n#endif\n  switch (error) {\n    case ncclUnhandledCudaError:\n      interpret = \"ncclUnhandledCudaError: Call to CUDA function failed.\";\n      break;\n    case ncclSystemError:\n      interpret =\n          \"ncclSystemError: System call (e.g. socket, malloc) or external library call failed or device error. \";\n#ifndef NCCL_REMOTE_ERROR\n      // Before ncclRemoteError was created, unexpected remote disconnect was\n      // categorized as ncclSystemError\n      interpret += \"It can be also caused by unexpected exit of a remote peer.\";\n#endif\n      break;\n    case ncclInternalError:\n      interpret = \"ncclInternalError: Internal check failed.\";\n      break;\n    case ncclInvalidArgument:\n      interpret = \"ncclInvalidArgument: Invalid value for an argument.\";\n      break;\n    case ncclInvalidUsage:\n      interpret =\n          \"ncclInvalidUsage: This usually reflects invalid usage of NCCL library.\";\n      break;\n#ifdef NCCL_REMOTE_ERROR\n    case ncclRemoteError:\n      interpret =\n          \"ncclRemoteError: A call failed possibly due to a network error or a remote process exiting prematurely.\";\n      break;\n#endif\n    default:\n      interpret = \"Unknown NCCL error!\";\n  }\n  return interpret + err;\n}\n\n// Helper function that gets the data type and issues error if not supported\nncclDataType_t getNcclDataType(at::ScalarType type) {\n  auto it = ncclDataType.find(type);\n  TORCH_CHECK_WITH(\n      TypeError,\n      it != ncclDataType.end(),\n      \"Input tensor data type is not supported for NCCL process group: \",\n      type);\n  return it->second;\n}\n\n// Dump proxyTrace log to stdout\nvoid printNcclCommProxyTrace(\n    const std::string& dumpReason,\n    const std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string>& dumpMap) {\n  LOG(INFO) << \"Dumping nccl comm trace, reason: \" << dumpReason;\n  for (auto& [key, value] : dumpMap) {\n    LOG(INFO) << \"key: \" << key << \", value: \" << value;\n  }\n  LOG(INFO) << \"----------------------\";\n}\n\n} // namespace c10d\n\n#endif // USE_C10D_NCCL\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " #ifndef NCCL_HAS_COMM_NONBLOCKING",
        "   C10D_NCCL_CHECK(::ncclCommAbort(ncclComm_), commFailureReason_);",
        " #else",
        "-  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_TIMEOUT(",
        "-      ::ncclCommAbort(ncclComm_), ncclComm_, commFailureReason_);",
        "+  // Inline polling with mutex already held.",
        "+  // We already hold the mutex_ lock here, so the direct call to",
        "+  // ncclCommGetAsyncError is safe.",
        "+  {",
        "+    ncclResult_t result = ::ncclCommAbort(ncclComm_);",
        "+    auto startTimepoint = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();",
        "+    auto timeout = nccl_nonblocking_timeout();",
        "+    while (result == ncclInProgress) {",
        "+      C10D_CHECK_TIMEOUT(startTimepoint, timeout);",
        "+      sched_yield();",
        "+      ncclCommGetAsyncError(ncclComm_, &result);",
        "+    }",
        "+    if (result != ncclSuccess) {",
        "+      std::string err = \"NCCL error in: \" + std::string(__FILE__) + \":\" +",
        "+          std::to_string(__LINE__) + \", \" + ncclGetErrorWithVersion(result) +",
        "+          \"\\n\" + getNcclErrorDetailStr(result, commFailureReason_);",
        "+      TORCH_CHECK_WITH(DistBackendError, false, err);",
        "+    }",
        "+  }",
        " #endif",
        "   aborted_ = true;",
        "   ncclComm_ = nullptr;"
      ],
      "newLines": 25,
      "newStart": 391,
      "oldLines": 8,
      "oldStart": 391
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "// tensor as input and output, we need to do a hack to get the first element in\n// the vector and pass it to fn.\n// TODO: we should clean up this in future (by either entirely removing lambda's\n// or removing input and output from lambda's signature).\n#ifndef NCCL_HAS_COMM_NONBLOCKING\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK(\n      fn(inputs[0], outputs[0], comm, ncclStream),\n      ncclComm->getNcclCommFailureReason());\n#else\n  C10D_NCCL_CHECK_TIMEOUT(\n      fn(inputs[0], outputs[0], comm, ncclStream),\n      comm,\n      ncclComm->getNcclCommFailureReason());\n#endif // NCCL_HAS_COMM_NONBLOCKING\n\n  post(ncclStream, work);\n\n  // End event should only be recorded after the ncclGroupEnd()\n  if (!coalescing_state_) {\n    work->ncclEndEvent_->record(ncclStream);\n  }\n  work->ncclComm_ = ncclComm;\n\n  {\n    c10::cuda::CUDAMultiStreamGuard streamGuard(ncclStream);",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/ProcessGroupNCCL.cpp",
    "numLines": 25,
    "startLine": 3745,
    "totalLines": 5988
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "originalFile": "#ifdef USE_C10D_NCCL\n\n#include <nlohmann/json.hpp>\n#include <exception>\n#include <map>\n#include <mutex>\n#include <sstream>\n#include <stdexcept>\n#include <tuple>\n#include <c10/util/thread_name.h>\n#include <torch/csrc/cuda/CUDAPluggableAllocator.h>\n#include <torch/csrc/cuda/nccl.h>\n#include <torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/FlightRecorder.hpp>\n#include <torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/NCCLUtils.hpp>\n#include <torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/NanCheck.hpp>\n#include <torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/ParamCommsUtils.hpp>\n#include <torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/PrefixStore.hpp>\n#include <torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/ProcessGroupNCCL.hpp>\n#include <torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/TraceUtils.h>\n#include <torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/Utils.hpp>\n#include <torch/csrc/distributed/c10d/cuda/utils.hpp>\n#include <torch/torch.h>\n#include <optional>\n\nnamespace c10d {\n\nconstexpr const char* const kNCCLAbortedCommStoreKey = \"NCCLABORTEDCOMM\";\nusing FlightRecorderCUDA = FlightRecorder<at::cuda::CUDAEvent>;\n\nnamespace {\n\n#if defined(NCCL_MAJOR) && \\\n    ((NCCL_MAJOR > 2) || (NCCL_MAJOR == 2) && (NCCL_MINOR >= 10))\n#define NCCL_HAS_AVG 1\n#endif // NCCL version >= 2.10\n\n// NCCL op mapping\nconst std::map<ReduceOp::RedOpType, ncclRedOp_t> ncclOp = {\n    {ReduceOp::MIN, ncclMin},\n    {ReduceOp::MAX, ncclMax},\n    {ReduceOp::SUM, ncclSum},\n    {ReduceOp::PRODUCT, ncclProd},\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_AVG\n    {ReduceOp::AVG, ncclAvg},\n#endif // NCCL_HAS_AVG\n};\n\ninline bool isUnsupportedFloat8(at::ScalarType t) {\n  return (\n      t == at::ScalarType::Float8_e5m2fnuz ||\n      t == at::ScalarType::Float8_e4m3fnuz ||\n      t == at::ScalarType::Float8_e8m0fnu\n#ifndef NCCL_SUPPORTS_FP8\n      || t == at::ScalarType::Float8_e5m2 || t == at::ScalarType::Float8_e4m3fn\n#endif\n  );\n}\n\n#ifdef ENABLE_NCCL_PREMUL_SUM_SUPPORT\ntemplate <typename T, ncclDataType_t dataType>\nncclRedOpRAII unpackPreMulSum(\n    const ReduceOp& reduceOp,\n    const ncclComm_t& comm) {\n  const auto* preMulSupplement =\n      reinterpret_cast<NCCLPreMulSumSupplement*>(reduceOp.supplement_.get());\n  ncclRedOp_t preMulSum{};\n  bool has_tensor = preMulSupplement->tensor_factor.defined();\n  auto residence = has_tensor ? ncclScalarDevice : ncclScalarHostImmediate;\n  const T* ptr_factor = has_tensor\n      ? preMulSupplement->tensor_factor.const_data_ptr<T>()\n      : nullptr;\n  T scalar_factor = T(preMulSupplement->double_factor);\n  ncclRedOpCreatePreMulSum(\n      &preMulSum,\n      // https://docs.nvidia.com/deeplearning/nccl/user-guide/docs/api/ops.html#ncclredopcreatepremulsum\n      // tells us that the scalar input is strictly a multiplier.\n      // NOLINTNEXTLINE(cppcoreguidelines-pro-type-const-cast)\n      /*scalar=*/has_tensor ? const_cast<T*>(ptr_factor) : &scalar_factor,\n      dataType,\n      residence,\n      comm);\n  return ncclRedOpRAII(preMulSum, comm);\n}\n#endif // ENABLE_NCCL_PREMUL_SUM_SUPPORT\n\nncclRedOpRAII getNcclReduceOp(\n    const ReduceOp& reduceOp,\n    at::Tensor& input,\n    const ncclDataType_t& dataType,\n    const ncclComm_t& comm) {\n  try {\n    if (input.scalar_type() == at::kBool) {\n      if (reduceOp == ReduceOp::SUM) {\n        // For bool tensors, map sum to max, which both represent a bitwise or.\n        // This is to prevent overflow issues with sum, since we use uint8 to\n        // represent a bool (see ncclDataType mapping).\n        return ncclMax;\n      }\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_AVG\n      if (reduceOp == ReduceOp::AVG) {\n        C10_THROW_ERROR(\n            TypeError, \"Cannot use ReduceOp.AVG with boolean inputs\");\n      }\n#endif // NCCL_HAS_AVG\n    }\n    if (reduceOp == ReduceOp::PREMUL_SUM) {\n#ifdef ENABLE_NCCL_PREMUL_SUM_SUPPORT\n      switch (dataType) {\n        case ncclHalf:\n          return unpackPreMulSum<at::Half, ncclHalf>(reduceOp, comm);\n        case ncclFloat:\n          return unpackPreMulSum<float, ncclFloat>(reduceOp, comm);\n        case ncclBfloat16:\n          return unpackPreMulSum<float, ncclBfloat16>(reduceOp, comm);\n        case ncclDouble:\n          return unpackPreMulSum<double, ncclDouble>(reduceOp, comm);\n        default:\n          C10_THROW_ERROR(\n              TypeError,\n              \"PreMulSum Data type must be half, float, bfloat16 or double\");\n          return ncclRedOp_t{};\n      }\n#else\n      C10_THROW_ERROR(ValueError, \"PreMulSum requires NCCL>=2.11.1\");\n#endif // ENABLE_NCCL_PREMUL_SUM_SUPPORT\n    }\n    return ncclOp.at(reduceOp);\n  } catch (const std::out_of_range&) {\n    switch (reduceOp) {\n      case ReduceOp::AVG:\n        C10_THROW_ERROR(\n            ValueError,\n            c10::str(\n                \"AVG requires NCCL 2.10+. The current version is \",\n                NCCL_MAJOR,\n                \".\",\n                NCCL_MINOR));\n        break;\n      case ReduceOp::BAND:\n        C10_THROW_ERROR(ValueError, \"Cannot use ReduceOp.BAND with NCCL\");\n        break;\n      case ReduceOp::BOR:\n        C10_THROW_ERROR(ValueError, \"Cannot use ReduceOp.BOR with NCCL\");\n        break;\n      case ReduceOp::BXOR:\n        C10_THROW_ERROR(ValueError, \"Cannot use ReduceOp.BXOR with NCCL\");\n        break;\n      default:\n        C10_THROW_ERROR(ValueError, \"Unhandled ReduceOp\");\n        break;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n// Get a key string from device\ninline std::string getKeyFromDevice(const at::Device& device) {\n  return std::to_string(device.index());\n}\n\nstd::string getKeySendRecv(int myRank, int peer) {\n  int lowRank = myRank < peer ? myRank : peer;\n  int highRank = myRank < peer ? peer : myRank;\n  std::string sendRecvPair =\n      std::to_string(lowRank) + \":\" + std::to_string(highRank);\n  return sendRecvPair;\n}\n\n// Get device from tensor\ninline at::Device getDevice(at::Tensor& tensor) {\n  return tensor.device();\n}\n\n// [Sync Streams] Helper that lets the input ncclStreams to wait for the current\n// stream. NCCL communications run on ncclStreams, but input tensors are\n// allocated on different streams (i.e., current streams). Communications on\n// ncclStreams cannot start before pending input tensor ops on current streams\n// finish. Otherwise, ops on two streams might read/write same tensors\n// concurrently.\n//\n// The synchronization above alone is not enough. We also need to make sure\n// input tensors are not freed before their usages on ncclStreams finish. This\n// can be achieved by calling c10::cuda::CUDACachingAllocator::recordStream,\n// which remembers the usage stream (ncclStream), creates an event on the usage\n// stream when GC attempts to free the input tensor, and delays GC until that\n// event is done.\nvoid syncStream(\n    at::Device& device,\n    at::cuda::CUDAEvent& ncclEvent,\n    at::cuda::CUDAStream& ncclStream) {\n  ncclEvent.record(at::cuda::getCurrentCUDAStream(device.index()));\n  ncclEvent.block(ncclStream);\n}\n\nstd::string getNcclAbortedCommStoreKey(const std::string& ncclIdStr) {\n  return std::string(kNCCLAbortedCommStoreKey) + \":\" + ncclIdStr;\n}\n\n// Returns exception's what() given an exception_ptr instance.\nstd::string getExceptionMsgFromExceptionPtr(\n    const std::exception_ptr& exceptionPtr) {\n  TORCH_CHECK(exceptionPtr != nullptr);\n  try {\n    std::rethrow_exception(exceptionPtr);\n  } catch (const std::exception& e) {\n    return e.what();\n  } catch (...) {\n    return \"Unknown exception type\";\n  }\n}\n\ninline void errorIfCapturingNonCapturableNCCL(c10::cuda::CaptureStatus status) {\n  // parentheses avoid some compiler warnings\n  static const uint64_t min_version =\n      (((uint64_t)2) << 32) + (((uint64_t)9) << 16) + ((uint64_t)6);\n  static const uint64_t cur_version = torch::cuda::nccl::version();\n  if (cur_version < min_version) {\n    TORCH_CHECK_WITH(\n        NotImplementedError,\n        status == c10::cuda::CaptureStatus::None,\n        \"Capturing NCCL collectives is only allowed with NCCL >= 2.9.6\");\n  }\n}\n\n// When TORCH_NCCL_USE_TENSOR_REGISTER_ALLOCATOR_HOOK is set, all tensors (no\n// matter how they have been allocated) are registered with all NCCL comms.\nbool shouldAllCommunicatorsRegisterAllTensors() {\n#ifdef NCCL_HAS_COMM_REGISTER\n  static const bool flag = [] {\n    const bool flag =\n        getCvarBool(TORCH_NCCL_USE_TENSOR_REGISTER_ALLOCATOR_HOOK, false);\n    if (flag &&\n        c10::cuda::CUDACachingAllocator::CUDAAllocatorConfig::\n            expandable_segments()) {\n      LOG(INFO)\n          << \"disables TORCH_NCCL_USE_TENSOR_REGISTER_ALLOCATOR_HOOK because it is not compatible with CUDA allocator expandable segments mode.\";\n      return false;\n    }\n    return flag;\n  }();\n  return flag;\n#else\n  return false;\n#endif // NCCL_HAS_COMM_REGISTER\n}\n\n} // namespace\n\n// Map each communicator to the memory pools registered with it.\n// This map is used when the caching allocator allocates or frees segments, in\n// order to register or deregister them with the relevant NCCL communicators.\n// There are two modes to do so:\n// - If TORCH_NCCL_USE_TENSOR_REGISTER_ALLOCATOR_HOOK=1 then *ALL* segments\n//   will be registered with *ALL* NCCL communicators (for the same device),\n//   even if they were allocated with cudaMalloc (which NCCL doesn't like).\n// - If a MemPool is explicitly registered with a ProcessGroup, then all its\n//   segments (current and future) will be registered with the NCCL communicator\n//   corresponding to the pool's device. This works best if the MemPool is set\n//   up to use ncclMemAlloc (which is exposed by the ProcessGroup).\n// Implementation notes:\n// - We cannot reuse devNCCLCommMap_ in each ProcessGroup because the key may be\n//   ranks rather than device in point-to-point case.\n// - This map has also to be maintained as global variable since the register\n//   hooks are called outside the scope of any PG, thus we need traverse\n//   communicators in all PGs.\n\n// MemPoolSet has ids of mempools used with this communicator, and whether they\n// were registered with window APIs or not\nusing MemPoolSet = std::unordered_set<\n    std::tuple<c10::cuda::MempoolId_t, bool>,\n    c10::hash<std::tuple<c10::cuda::MempoolId_t, bool>>>;\nstatic std::unordered_map<std::shared_ptr<NCCLComm>, MemPoolSet>\n    ncclCommMemPoolMap;\nstatic std::mutex ncclCommMemPoolMapMutex;\n\nstd::atomic<bool> ProcessGroupNCCL::shouldDump_(false);\n\nstatic void cacheAllocatorRegisterHook(\n    const c10::cuda::CUDACachingAllocator::TraceEntry& te) {\n  // Register after SEGMENT_ALLOC\n  if (te.action_ !=\n      c10::cuda::CUDACachingAllocator::TraceEntry::Action::SEGMENT_ALLOC) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(ncclCommMemPoolMapMutex);\n  for (auto& [ncclComm, memPools] : ncclCommMemPoolMap) {\n    if (te.device_ == ncclComm->getDeviceIndex()) {\n      bool symm = false;\n      bool should_register = shouldAllCommunicatorsRegisterAllTensors();\n      auto it =\n          std::find_if(memPools.begin(), memPools.end(), [&](const auto& tup) {\n            return std::get<0>(tup) == te.mempool_;\n          });\n      if (it != memPools.end()) {\n        should_register = true;\n        symm = std::get<1>(*it);\n      }\n      if (should_register) {\n        // NOLINTNEXTLINE(performance-no-int-to-ptr)\n        ncclComm->registerSegment(\n            reinterpret_cast<void*>(te.addr_),\n            te.size_,\n            /*errorOnRereg*/ false,\n            /*window*/ symm);\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nstatic void cacheAllocatorDeregisterHook(\n    const c10::cuda::CUDACachingAllocator::TraceEntry& te) {\n  // deregister before SEGMENT_FREE\n  if (te.action_ !=\n      c10::cuda::CUDACachingAllocator::TraceEntry::Action::SEGMENT_FREE) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(ncclCommMemPoolMapMutex);\n  for (auto& [ncclComm, memPools] : ncclCommMemPoolMap) {\n    if (te.device_ == ncclComm->getDeviceIndex()) {\n      bool symm = false;\n      bool should_register = shouldAllCommunicatorsRegisterAllTensors();\n      auto it =\n          std::find_if(memPools.begin(), memPools.end(), [&](const auto& tup) {\n            return std::get<0>(tup) == te.mempool_;\n          });\n      if (it != memPools.end()) {\n        should_register = true;\n        symm = std::get<1>(*it);\n      }\n      if (should_register) {\n        // NOLINTNEXTLINE(performance-no-int-to-ptr)\n        ncclComm->deregisterSegment(reinterpret_cast<void*>(te.addr_), symm);\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nstatic void attachAllocatorHooks() {\n  static auto flag [[maybe_unused]] = [] {\n    // Attaching hooks fails if CUDACachingAllocator is not initialized, so\n    // Init for CUDA is called (and is a no-op if CUDA is already\n    // initialized).\n    at::globalContext().lazyInitDevice(c10::DeviceType::CUDA);\n    c10::cuda::CUDACachingAllocator::attachAllocatorTraceTracker(\n        &cacheAllocatorRegisterHook);\n    c10::cuda::CUDACachingAllocator::attachAllocatorTraceTracker(\n        &cacheAllocatorDeregisterHook);\n    return true;\n  }();\n}\n\nstatic std::\n    unordered_map<std::string, std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string>>\n    getNCCLCommDumpMap() {\n#if (defined(IS_NCCLX) || defined(USE_ROCM)) && defined(NCCL_COMM_DUMP)\n  std::unordered_map<\n      std::string /* ncclUniqueID */,\n      std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string> /* dump from this comm */>\n      ncclDumpMap;\n  // dump_nccl_trace is only called from the default PG (local_id_=0), but we\n  // want to dump from all comms so we need to iterate over ncclCommMemPoolMap,\n  // which is static\n  std::vector<std::shared_ptr<NCCLComm>> allNCCLComms;\n  // within the critical section, we don't want to dump while holding the lock\n  // as dump might hang\n  {\n    std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(ncclCommMemPoolMapMutex);\n    for (auto& [ncclComm, _] : ncclCommMemPoolMap) {\n      allNCCLComms.push_back(ncclComm);\n    }\n  }\n  for (auto& ncclComm : allNCCLComms) {\n    ncclDumpMap[ncclComm->getUniqueHash()] = ncclComm->ncclCommDump();\n  }\n  return ncclDumpMap;\n#else\n  return std::unordered_map<\n      std::string,\n      std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string>>();\n#endif // (defined(IS_NCCLX) || defined(USE_ROCM)) && defined(NCCL_COMM_DUMP)\n}\n\nvoid reset_nccl_trace() {\n  FlightRecorderCUDA::get()->reset_all();\n}\n\nstd::string dump_nccl_trace(\n    bool includeCollectives,\n    bool includeStackTraces,\n    bool onlyActive) {\n  auto ncclDumpMap = getNCCLCommDumpMap();\n#if defined(USE_ROCM) && defined(NCCL_COMM_DUMP)\n  for (const auto& [ncclUniqueIDStr, dump] : ncclDumpMap) {\n    printNcclCommProxyTrace(\"Received dump signal \" + ncclUniqueIDStr, dump);\n  }\n#endif // defined(USE_ROCM) && defined(NCCL_COMM_DUMP)\n  return FlightRecorderCUDA::get()->dump(\n      ncclDumpMap, includeCollectives, includeStackTraces, onlyActive);\n}\n\nstd::string dump_nccl_trace_json(bool includeCollectives, bool onlyActive) {\n  auto ncclDumpMap = getNCCLCommDumpMap();\n  return FlightRecorderCUDA::get()->dump_json(\n      ncclDumpMap, includeCollectives, onlyActive);\n}\n\nstd::optional<std::function<void(std::function<void(const std::string&)>)>>&\nget_cpp_trace_dumper() {\n  static std::optional<\n      std::function<void(std::function<void(const std::string&)>)>>\n      dumper(std::nullopt);\n  return dumper;\n}\n\ngil_checker_t& get_gil_checker() {\n  static gil_checker_t gil_checker = nullptr;\n  return gil_checker;\n}\n\nstatic std::future<bool> launchAsyncGilCheck() {\n  std::promise<bool> resultPromise;\n  std::future<bool> resultFuture = resultPromise.get_future();\n  TORCH_CHECK(get_gil_checker(), \"Can't check GIL with null GIL checker\");\n  std::thread workerThread([promise = std::move(resultPromise)]() mutable {\n    c10::setThreadName(\"pt_nccl_gil_chk\");\n\n    try {\n      auto& gil_checker = get_gil_checker();\n      promise.set_value((*gil_checker)());\n    } catch (...) {\n      promise.set_exception(std::current_exception());\n    }\n  });\n\n  // Detach the thread to allow it to run independently\n  workerThread.detach();\n\n  return resultFuture;\n}\n\nconst int64_t ProcessGroupNCCL::kWatchdogThreadSleepMillis = 100;\nconstexpr int64_t kSynchronizeBusyWaitMillis = 1;\nthread_local uint64_t ProcessGroupNCCL::ncclActiveGroupCounter_ = 0;\n\nstd::ostream& operator<<(\n    std::ostream& output,\n    const ProcessGroupNCCL::WorkNCCL& workNCCL) {\n  std::string workInfo;\n  workInfo = c10::str(\n      \"WorkNCCL(\",\n      \"SeqNum=\",\n      workNCCL.seq_,\n      \", OpType=\",\n      opTypeToString(workNCCL.opType_),\n      \", NumelIn=\",\n      workNCCL.numelIn_,\n      \", NumelOut=\",\n      workNCCL.numelOut_,\n      \", Timeout(ms)=\",\n      workNCCL.opTimeout_.count(),\n      \")\");\n  return output << workInfo;\n}\n\n/* Implementation of TensorShelf class */\n\nvoid TensorShelf::stash(std::vector<at::Tensor>& tensors) {\n  std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);\n  tVector_.insert(tVector_.end(), tensors.begin(), tensors.end());\n}\n\nvoid TensorShelf::stash(TensorShelf& other) {\n  std::vector<at::Tensor>& otherVec = other.get();\n  this->stash(otherVec);\n}\n\nvoid TensorShelf::unstash() {\n  this->clear();\n}\n\nbool TensorShelf::empty() {\n  std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);\n  return tVector_.empty();\n}\n\nvoid TensorShelf::clear() {\n  std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);\n  tVector_.clear();\n}\n\nstd::vector<at::Tensor>& TensorShelf::get() {\n  return tVector_;\n}\n\nProcessGroupNCCL::WorkNCCL::WorkNCCL(\n    std::string pgUID,\n    std::string pgDesc,\n    at::Device& device,\n    int rank,\n    OpType opType,\n    uint64_t seq,\n    bool isP2P,\n    const char* profilingTitle,\n    const std::optional<std::vector<at::Tensor>>& inputs,\n    bool enableTiming,\n    bool cudaEventCacheEnabled,\n    DebugLevel distDebugLevel)\n    : Work(rank, opType, profilingTitle, inputs),\n      pgUID_(std::move(pgUID)),\n      pgDesc_(std::move(pgDesc)),\n      device_(device),\n      workStartTime_(std::chrono::steady_clock::now()),\n      seq_(seq),\n      isP2P_(isP2P),\n      timingEnabled_(enableTiming),\n      distDebugLevel_(distDebugLevel) {\n  // Creates the CUDA event wrappers\n  // Note: The actual events are lazily created when first recorded to with\n  // DEFAULT_FLAGS = cudaEventDisableTiming.\n  if (cudaEventCacheEnabled) {\n    ncclStartEvent_ = enableTiming\n        ?
